title: error tape (regenerated class)
manager: The error tape — a recorded stream of the errors a machine has produced, in the same tape family as input and output — so a run's failures are a replayable, inspectable artifact rather than lost console output. This is the diagnostics substrate: a bug report can ship the error tape, and the whole run can be replayed to reproduce exactly how the machine arrived at the failure, which is the difference between a fixable bug and a shrug.
cs: error tape in the tape family (§11/§14); regenerated class; replayable; pairs with the output/input tapes (W7.9) and the era-4 flight recorder.
